questions.subtitle: Let us know so we can tailor the results.

options.description: I'm a worker looking for services on behalf of someone

options.label: Myself
options.description: I'm looking for support for myself

options.label: A friend or family member
options.description: I'm looking for services to help someone I know

questions.subtitle: Choose the area that best describes the situation.

questions.title: What kind of support do you need?

options.label: Food assistance
options.description: Food hampers and emergency food relief

questions.title: What area of rights or advocacy?

options.label: Policy & advocacy
options.description: Community rights, policy submissions, and government engagement

options.label: Voting & elections
options.description: Non-partisan voter education and political engagement

options.label: Prison & youth chaplaincy
options.description: Spiritual guidance and rehabilitation in correctional facilities

options.description: Daily prayers, Jummah, and Eid at ICV City Mosque

services.name: Food Hamper Program
services.description: The Food Hamper Program provides essential food relief to families and individuals experiencing food insecurity. Hampers include culturally appropriate halal food items and essential groceries. Emergency hampers are available on request.
services.eligibility: Available to individuals and families in need within Victoria. No documentation required for emergency hampers.
eligibilityList: Located in Metropolitan Melbourne and regional Victoria.
Individuals and families experiencing food insecurity.
No documentation required for emergency hampers.
services.hours: Distribution events held monthly. Emergency hampers available weekdays 9 AM – 5 PM.

services.name: Emergency Relief
services.description: Emergency relief is available for individuals and families experiencing hardship. Aid includes food vouchers and food hampers. Support is provided via the emergency assistance telephone line.
services.status: Wednesday from 9:00 AM until 4:00 PM (Emergency Assistance Line.)
eligibilityList: Located in Hume local government area.
Must provide proof of identification and Health Care Card.
Assistance is only provided in emergencies and following an assessment interview.

services.name: Doorways Emergency Relief
services.organisation: Salvation Army, Craigieburn Doorways Community Support Services
services.description: Doorways provides emergency relief and holistic case management, with referral to internal and external support services, for singles and families experiencing disadvantage.
eligibilityList: Located in Attwood, Broadmeadows, Campbellfield, Coolaroo, Craigieburn, Dallas, Gladstone Park, Greenvale, Meadow Heights, Roxburgh Park, Westmeadows, Somerton and Yuroke.

services.name: Emergency Relief
services.description: Emergency relief is available for individuals and families experiencing financial hardship. Aid includes food parcels, food vouchers, fuel vouchers and financial assistance for prescription medications.
eligibilityList: Located in Postcodes 3063, 3064 and 3756 which includes Oaklands Junction, Yuroke, Craigieburn, Donnybrook, Kalkallo, Wallan, Upper Plenty, Darraweit Guim and Chintin in the Hume, Mitchell and Macedon Ranges local government areas.
People experiencing financial hardship living in postcodes 3063, 3064 and 3756.

services.name: Emergency Relief: Food Vouchers
services.description: Emergency relief is available for individuals and families from the Latin American ethnic community who are experiencing disadvantage. Aid includes food vouchers. Referrals to other services may also be provided.
eligibilityList: Individuals and families from the Latin American community experiencing disadvantage.

services.name: Financial Aid
services.description: Provides financial aid for current and former Australian Defence Force (ADF) members and their families who are experiencing financial hardship. Support includes emergency financial assistance, budgeting help, and referrals to specialist services.
eligibilityList: Australian Defence Force (ADF) members and their families who are experiencing financial hardship.
Referred by Self, family or agency.

services.name: Emergency Relief
services.description: Emergency relief is available for individuals and families experiencing disadvantage. Aid includes food vouchers and other vouchers which are available upon assessment.

services.name: Interest Free Loans
services.description: Interest-free loans to help individuals and families cover essential expenses such as rent, utilities, medical costs, and education fees. Loans are provided in accordance with Islamic principles.
services.eligibility: Victorian residents experiencing financial hardship. Proof of income and identity required.
eligibilityList: Proof of income and identity required.
Must be able to demonstrate capacity to repay.

services.name: No Interest Loan Scheme (NILS)
services.description: No Interest Loans of up to $2,000 for essential goods and services including whitegoods, furniture, education costs, medical expenses, and car repairs. Loans are interest-free and fee-free.
eligibilityList: Must hold a Health Care Card or Pension Card.
Must have lived at current address for at least 3 months.
Individual or combined household income under $70,000 per year.

services.name: Financial Counselling
services.description: Free financial counselling for people experiencing financial difficulty. Support includes budgeting advice, negotiating with creditors, understanding rights and options, and referrals to emergency relief and other services.
services.address: Suite 6, 1 Whitehorse Road, Box Hill VIC 3128

services.name: Family Violence Program
services.description: Culturally appropriate education, support, and referral services for families affected by family violence. The program provides early intervention and connects people with specialist support services.
services.eligibility: Open to all community members. Confidential service.
eligibilityList: Referred by Self, family, or agency.

services.name: Family Violence Response
services.description: Specialist family violence support including crisis response, safety planning, case management, and court support. Provides culturally responsive services and can connect with interpreter services.
eligibilityList: Women, children and families experiencing family violence.
Men seeking support to change their behaviour.
Referred by Self, police, or agency.

services.name: Family Violence Outreach
services.description: Community-based family violence outreach providing case management, risk assessment, safety planning, and connections to housing, legal, and financial support services.
eligibilityList: Individuals and families experiencing or at risk of family violence.

services.name: Islamophobia Services
services.description: Direct case support for individuals who have experienced Islamophobia, racism, or religious discrimination. We provide confidential case management, legal referrals, counselling support, and help with reporting to authorities.
eligibilityList: Open to anyone who has experienced Islamophobia or religious discrimination in Victoria.

services.name: Discrimination Enquiry Line
services.organisation: Victorian Equal Opportunity & Human Rights Commission
services.description: Free, confidential service to discuss experiences of discrimination, sexual harassment, victimisation or racial or religious vilification. Can assist with making a formal complaint and provides information about rights under the Equal Opportunity Act.
eligibilityList: Anyone who has experienced discrimination, harassment, or vilification.

services.name: Islamic Counselling Services
services.organisation: Australian Muslim Women's Centre for Human Rights
services.description: Culturally and religiously sensitive counselling for Muslim individuals affected by racism, discrimination, or community-based trauma. Counsellors understand the specific impacts of Islamophobia and provide a safe space for healing.

services.name: Mental Health & Wellbeing Services
services.description: Community-based mental health support including counselling, group programs, and clinical services. Provides culturally responsive care with access to interpreters and bilingual staff.
eligibilityList: No referral needed for initial assessment.
Referred by Self, GP, or agency.

services.name: Community Health & Wellbeing
services.description: Comprehensive health services including counselling, allied health, chronic disease management, and community health programs. Provides culturally safe care for diverse communities in Melbourne's west.
eligibilityList: Located in Melton, Bacchus Marsh, and surrounding areas.

services.name: Advocacy
services.description: ICV advocates for the rights of the Victorian Muslim community through government engagement at all levels, policy submissions, media responses, and representation on issues including discrimination, religious freedom, and social inclusion.

services.name: Hospital Chaplaincy Program
services.description: Spiritual and pastoral care for Muslim patients in hospitals across Victoria. Trained chaplains offer emotional support, prayer, religious guidance, and practical assistance to patients and their families.

services.name: Prison & Youth Chaplaincy
services.description: Spiritual guidance, counselling, and rehabilitation support for Muslim individuals in correctional facilities across Victoria. Our chaplains work with both adult and youth offenders.

services.name: Muslim Connect Program
services.description: Support for individuals transitioning from correctional facilities back into the community. Includes pre-release planning, post-release mentoring, housing assistance, and employment support.
services.eligibility: Muslim individuals in or exiting correctional facilities in Victoria.
eligibilityList: Muslim individuals in or exiting correctional facilities in Victoria.
Referred by Corrections Victoria or self.

services.name: My Vote Matters Program
services.description: Non-partisan voter education providing information about political parties' positions on issues relevant to the Muslim community, voter education resources, and opportunities to engage with elected representatives.

services.name: Scholarships Programs
services.description: Scholarships for Victorian Muslim students pursuing higher education and professional development. Available for undergraduate and postgraduate studies across a range of disciplines.
services.eligibility: Victorian Muslim students enrolled in or applying to accredited educational institutions.
eligibilityList: Enrolled in or applying to accredited educational institutions.

services.name: Skills & Training Programs
services.description: Vocational training and skills development programs including Certificate and Diploma courses. Specialises in community services, aged care, disability, and early childhood education with culturally inclusive delivery.
eligibilityList: Australian citizens, permanent residents, and eligible visa holders.
Government-subsidised places available for eligible students.

services.name: Skills First — Free TAFE
services.description: Free TAFE courses and subsidised training across hundreds of courses. Priority courses include community services, health, early childhood, IT, and trades. Fee-free for eligible students.
services.status: Enquiry line open weekdays 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
eligibilityList: Australian citizens, permanent residents, and eligible visa holders.

services.name: ICV City Mosque
services.description: The ICV City Mosque provides daily prayer services for the Melbourne CBD community including five daily prayers, Jummah (Friday) prayers, Taraweeh during Ramadan, and Eid prayers.
services.hours: Open daily for five prayer times. Jummah: 1:15 PM & 1:45 PM.
services.status: Open for Dhuhr prayer at 1:15 PM

services.name: Member Society Engagement
services.description: ICV connects and supports 70+ member societies across Victoria through regular consultations, capacity-building workshops, and resource sharing.
